title: We’re So Back … With a Movie Mega-Mailbag!
format: Amanda's back from vacation and the two catch up on the Twisters box-office win and the Deadpool & Wolverine landing next weekend, pan Fly Me to the Moon, then open a mega-mailbag: 70mm road trips, theater etiquette, underused actors, a Wes-or-PTA Bond, pandemic castaways, and the film books worth reading.

segments.heading: The summer box office
segments.note: Twisters, Longlegs, and the Deadpool & Wolverine looming next weekend

films.title: Twisters
films.note: $80.5M domestic opening, the biggest ever for a natural-disaster movie; Sean called it movie of the summer. Talking points: Glenn Powell's arrival as a poster-driving star, the kiss that was shot and cut, and the rain-soaked white-tee shot the editors fought to put back in for Lee Isaac Chung.

films.title: Twister
films.note: The original, invoked for Helen Hunt's outdoor costuming, which Daisy Edgar Jones's look echoes.

films.title: The Day After Tomorrow
films.note: The prior natural-disaster opening record at $68M, proof it's a small box-office sub-genre.

films.title: Titanic
films.note: The glacier-vs-iceberg correction, and a lesson in respecting nature.

films.title: Inside Out 2
films.note: The summer's billion-dollar movie, which takes the pressure off Deadpool & Wolverine.

films.title: Thelma
films.note: The summer's indie hit.

films.title: Longlegs
films.note: Neon's horror hit, already near $45M and closing on Parasite as the label's biggest ever. Directed by Oz Perkins, once the guy from Legally Blonde.

films.title: Deadpool & Wolverine
films.note: Out next weekend, and the two are seeing it that night. The Hugh Jackman suit reportedly had grown men crying on set.

films.title: Deadpool
films.note: Sean's verdict: okay. Fast-forward material.

films.title: Deadpool 2
films.note: Sean's verdict: legit good. Amanda's assigned homework.

films.title: Legally Blonde
films.note: Where Oz Perkins acted before the strange pivot to horror directing.

films.title: Casablanca
films.note: The implied-consummation defense: the light goes down and you understand what happens, no hardcore scene required.

films.title: Pride & Prejudice
films.note: Joe Wright's version, whose purer cut withholds the final kiss and works anyway.

films.title: Jurassic Park
films.note: Laura Dern's outdoor style, cited alongside Helen Hunt's as the Twisters look.

segments.heading: Aaron Sorkin's late period
segments.note: Prompted by his New York Times op-ed pitching a Biden exit and a Mitt Romney nomination

films.title: Molly's Game
films.note: Sean likes it a lot, but marks it the beginning of the end: Sorkin's point of view stops clicking with the world here.

films.title: The Trial of the Chicago 7
films.note: The second of the three he directed.

films.title: Being the Ricardos
films.note: The last of the three; none of them quite work.

films.title: Fly Me to the Moon
films.note: Scarlett Johansson as a marketing whiz brought in to sell the Apollo program to a skeptical public, Channing Tatum as the launch director she falls for, Woody Harrelson as the spook who wants a faked backup moon landing ready in case the real one fails. Directed by Greg Berlanti from Rose Gilroy's first produced script; originally set for Jason Bateman and Chris Evans. Both find it baffling and over-long at 132 minutes, a movie idea with TV pacing and no stakes, charming in patches but not connected. It got pummeled at the box office by Longlegs.

films.title: Apollo 13
films.note: Ron Howard's film that Sean loves deeply; Tatum's Apollo 1 trauma keeps brushing up against it.

films.title: Hillbilly Elegy
films.note: The Ron Howard Netflix No. 1 he seems to be backing away from, calling it 'absolutely reprehensible.'

segments.heading: Mailbag: cinema's future and a dream Cruise auteur
segments.note: Jose asks whether a bad 2025 kills cinema; Luke asks for Tom Cruise's dream director

films.title: Furiosa: A Mad Max Saga
films.note: Cited in the mix of good and disappointing to come.

films.title: F1
films.note: Cruise notably skipped the F1 grid; the theory is that only one movie star (Brad Pitt) could own the space to launch the film.

films.title: Days of Thunder
films.note: Cruise as more of a NASCAR guy than an F1 guy.

films.title: Thunderbolts
films.note: The Florence Pugh Marvel movie. 'I hope Florence Pugh got a lot of money.'

films.title: Mickey 17
films.note: New-dad Robert Pattinson; Amanda hopes to be back from leave by then.

films.title: Avatar: Fire and Ash
films.note: Dated December 19, 2025. Sean's skeptical it holds, and producer Jon Landau's death may affect the release.

films.title: Gravity
films.note: Alfonso Cuarón proving he can put a movie star in space; Sean's dream is a Cuarón-Cruise film, or better, a Cruise-Tarantino one.

films.title: Roma
films.note: Sean likes Cuarón in both mainstream and this mode.

films.title: Eyes Wide Shut
films.note: Amanda's pick is Fincher directing Cruise, two obsessive types in that vein.

segments.heading: Mailbag: the theater experience
segments.note: 70mm road trips, phone etiquette, the worst snack, and the ideal seat

films.title: Dune: Part Two
films.note: The 2024 film most worth driving to a true IMAX for.

films.title: Oppenheimer
films.note: Shot for 70mm IMAX; Sean's whole experience of it changed for the better going from Dolby to IMAX.

films.title: Civil War
films.note: No 70mm print, but the kind of big, loud movie you'd want as large as possible.

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: Seen on 70mm at the Egyptian: the only Coppola movie that plays like a dream, in a theater where nobody would dare pull out a phone.

films.title: Vertigo
films.note: A 70mm date-night at the Egyptian during the American Cinematheque festival.

films.title: The Watchers
films.note: Sean saw it alone on a Sunday night, ideal for taking notes on his phone.

films.title: Killers of the Flower Moon
films.note: Charles Holmes's story of Alamo patrons stomping around for popcorn refills.

films.title: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire
films.note: Seen in ScreenX, occasion of the ill-fated sweet-tart-ropes purchase.

films.title: The Zone of Interest
films.note: The snack-bag-rustling offense; a movie you'd want zero people near you for.

films.title: The Beekeeper
films.note: The aisle-seat mix-up, and, per Amanda, 'fucking sick.'

films.title: Road House
films.note: The movie Sean was coming from when the Beekeeper seats got scrambled.

films.title: Ferrari
films.note: Part of the 'beekeeper-zone of interest guy' running bit.

films.title: Despicable Me 4
films.note: A movie for children; you won't find a phones-down crowd there.

segments.heading: Mailbag: underused actors, a Wes-or-PTA Bond, and pandemic castaways
segments.note: Eric on talent that hasn't broken out, Chris on a Wes-or-PTA Bond, Evan on straight-to-streaming misses

films.title: Waves
films.note: Taylor Russell, who's shown flashes but hasn't gotten the mainstream vehicle.

films.title: Bones and All
films.note: More Taylor Russell in the auteur lane.

films.title: Bodies Bodies Bodies
films.note: Myha'la doing interesting character work, but not yet a star-making performance.

films.title: Leave the World Behind
films.note: Myha'la again; Amanda wants a movie built around her.

films.title: Causeway
films.note: Brian Tyree Henry's supporting-nominated turn opposite Jennifer Lawrence.

films.title: If Beale Street Could Talk
films.note: Brian Tyree Henry gets 15 minutes of screen time and owns the movie.

films.title: Godzilla vs. Kong
films.note: Cited as proof Brian Tyree Henry is great in everything, running the Philip Seymour Hoffman playbook.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Kit Harington as the surprise Black Knight, Harry Styles's cameo, Brian Tyree Henry as the Oppenheimer of the group: a disaster, fondly recalled.

films.title: Skyfall
films.note: The prestige-Bond precedent that makes PTA the safer, and Wes the more meaningful, choice.

films.title: Back to Black
films.note: Jack O'Connell's 30 good seconds, floated among the interchangeable next-Bond contenders.

films.title: Inherent Vice
films.note: The PTA-in-70mm pick; Sean has tickets for August 4, home by 2am.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: The obvious No. 1 pandemic castaway: the most divisive Nolan, and Sean thinks it would've been much bigger seen together in theaters. Robert Pattinson forever.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: The $500M worldwide benchmark Tenet fell short of, pandemic notwithstanding.

films.title: Batman Begins
films.note: Tenet was Nolan's lowest-grossing movie since this one.

films.title: Man of Steel
films.note: The Nolan story-and-producer credit; Kevin Costner as Superman's dad, and it goes bad.

films.title: Soul
films.note: A Disney/Pixar movie that would have done better in normal times.

films.title: Raya and the Last Dragon
films.note: The pandemic Disney movie Sean thinks would have been a much bigger deal, later confirmed by a 3-year-old's themed birthday party.

films.title: Malignant
films.note: James Wan's horror swing, dumped day-and-date on Max under Project Popcorn.

films.title: Palm Springs
films.note: A Sundance hit that made money on Hulu but could have been a theater good time.

segments.heading: Mailbag: grown-up movies, baby names, and film books
segments.note: Scott on why adults love kids' movies, CJ on names ruined by characters, Busboom on becoming film-literate

films.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
films.note: Sean's thesis: a generation raised on this stuff grew up, got the power, and turned every childhood fascination into a movie.

films.title: The Bikeriders
films.note: One of the well-made adult movies to push friends toward, alongside Challengers and Longlegs.

films.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
films.note: Why Amanda can't name a kid Dickie: the fate of Dickie Greenleaf. Time for the annual rewatch.

films.title: The Town
films.note: Where Sean insists Blake Lively played a character with anxiety, in the great is-she-brat debate.

films.title: A Simple Favor
films.note: The Blake Lively movie tangled up with the 'A Simple Kind of Life' No Doubt song.

films.title: It Ends with Us
films.note: Blake Lively's Colleen Hoover adaptation this summer, to be covered around the Sing Sing episode.

films.title: Sing Sing
films.note: The upcoming Movies We Missed slot where It Ends With Us may land.

Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire.title: Godzilla x Kong: The New Empire
